zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Le Despenser於1400年7月26日出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根卡迪福時，她的父親Thomas Le Despencer Earl of Gloucster26歲，母親Constance of York26歲。她於1411年7月27日與Richard Beauchamp在英國武斯特斯爾亨利 卡素結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1439年12月27日在英國英格蘭倫敦享年39歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利福可斯波利 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Le Despenser于1400年7月26日出生在威尔士格拉莫爾根卡迪福时，她的父亲Thomas Le Despencer Earl of Gloucster26岁，母亲Constance of York26岁。她于1411年7月27日与Richard Beauchamp在英国武斯特斯爾亨利 卡素结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1439年12月27日在英国英格兰倫敦享年39岁，埋葬在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利福可斯波利 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Le Despenser was born on 26 July 1400, in Cardiff, Glamorgan, Wales, her father, Thomas Le Despencer Earl of Gloucster, was 26 and her mother, Constance of York, was 26. She married Richard Beauchamp on 27 July 1411, in Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 27 December 1439, in London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 39, and was buried in Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Le Despenser est née le 26 juillet 1400, à Cardiff, Glamorgan, Galles, son père, Thomas Le Despencer Earl of Gloucster, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Constance of York, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Beauchamp le 27 juillet 1411, à Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 27 décembre 1439, à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterrée à Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Le Despenser am 26. Juli 1400 in Cardiff, Glamorgan, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Le Despencer Earl of Gloucster 26 und ihre Mutter Constance of York 26. Sie hat am 27. Juli 1411 in Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, England Richard Beauchamp geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 27. Dezember 1439 im Alter von 39 Jahren in London, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Le Despenser è nata il il 26 luglio 1400 nel Cardiff, Glamorgan, Galles, sua padre, Thomas Le Despencer Earl of Gloucster, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Constance of York, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Richard Beauchamp il 27 luglio 1411, nel Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 27 dicembre 1439, nel London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolta a Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Le Despenserが1400年7月26日にウェールズグラモーガンカーディッフで生まれた時，父Thomas Le Despencer Earl of Gloucsterは26歳，母Constance of Yorkは26歳であった。1411年7月27日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアハンリー カストルでRichard Beauchampと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1439年12月27日にイギリスイギリスロンドンで39歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアテウケスブリーテウケスブリー アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.Plot: Beauchamp Chapel, Tewkesbury Abbey: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이 튜케스버이 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Le Despenser(이)가 1400년 7월 26일 웨일스 글램르건 카디프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Le Despencer Earl of Gloucster(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Constance of York(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1411년 7월 27일 영국 워스스터실르 핸리 카스틀에서 Richard Beauchamp(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1439년 12월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이 튜케스버이 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Le Despenser nasceu em 26 de julho de 1400, em Cardiff, Glamorgan, Gales, seu pai, Thomas Le Despencer Earl of Gloucster, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Constance of York, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard Beauchamp em 27 de julho de 1411, em Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 27 de dezembro de 1439, em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 39 anos, e foi sepultada em Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Plot: Beauchamp Chapel, Tewkesbury Abbey: тюкесбури эбби, тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Le Despenser родилась 26 июля 1400 г. в местности кардифф, глэморган, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Thomas Le Despencer Earl of Gloucster было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Constance of York, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Beauchamp 27 июля 1411 г. в местности ханли кастл, ворсестиршир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 27 декабря 1439 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 39 лет и была похоронена в местности тюкесбури эбби, тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel Le Despenser nació el 26 de julio de 1400, en Cardiff, Glamorgan, Gales, su papá, Thomas Le Despencer Earl of Gloucster, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Constance of York, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Richard Beauchamp el 27 de julio de 1411, en Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 27 de diciembre de 1439, en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultada en Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
